name: SOL Value Recovery Scale-In v1
edge_mechanism: scale-in around value recovery when confirmation improves across bars instead of one-shot all-in timing
thesis: SOL 4h is the best primary lane for testing whether value recovery is better expressed as staged participation across improving confirmation bars rather than a one-shot all-in reclaim. The edge is the progressive confirmation of recovery, not a single heroic reversal candle.
invalidation_condition: Abort if recovery setups remain too sparse or if staggered participation adds complexity without improving post-cost expectancy over a simpler reclaim.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price flushes below fast value, reclaims it, then continues to confirm recovery over the next bars, allowing staged participation into the repair move.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In chop, early recovery attempts often fail. Staging exposure only as confirmation improves can keep entries denser than rare perfect reversals while reducing commitment to weak first reclaim bars.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: SOL 4h is a strong lane because recoveries are visible, frequent enough to test, and not as noisy as lower intraday structure.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Recovery can reclaim value but fail to extend enough, leaving the added scale-in step as unnecessary complexity.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries are dense enough but later add-ons do not help, collapse the scale-in into a simpler two-stage management path before touching the entry grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A workable set of recovery trades where initial size earns most expectancy and added size helps only on stronger repairs.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: SOL 4h plus one cross-lane show credible density and better post-cost recovery behavior with improving-confirmation staging.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sparse mirage, or no improvement versus simpler reclaim behavior.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; reclaim, structure confirmation, and trend-break avoidance are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad recovery grammar rather than exact capitulation candle requirements

validation_basket.why_included: Best lane for staged value-recovery participation in the allowed basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: Recovery trades occur often enough and staged confirmation improves expectancy after costs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Recoveries are too sparse or scale-in adds no value.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Repair moves with improving confirmation across bars rather than all-or-nothing reversals.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same staged recovery logic survives on a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH preserves recovery behavior with acceptable density.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Slightly cleaner but less explosive recovery sequences than SOL.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same staged recovery idea survives on the faster adjacent lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: 1h keeps density without collapsing into churn.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More frequent but smaller recovery sequences.
